summary: PR #2427 added partial decompilation work for All-Star mode in src/melee/gm/gmallstar.c, including four previously stubbed functions, local data definitions for gm_803DEBE8 and gm_803DEC4C, include updates, and symbol-table split/size metadata for the new data object. The bot report recorded fuzzy-score improvements for gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, gm_801B5ACC, .data, and .bss, but also a regression in gm_801B60A4_OnLoad; the author described the result as a base for others rather than polished final source.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; added All-Star data definitions and implementations for gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, and gm_801B5ACC, plus edits in gm_801B60A4_OnLoad.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gm/gmallstar.c added 379 deleted 8 hunks 4
raw_diff: gmallstar.c added gm_803DEBE8, gm_803DEC4C, gm_80490940 and replaced stubs for gm_801B5324/gm_801B5624/gm_801B59AC/gm_801B5ACC
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists improvements for gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, gm_801B5ACC and regression for gm_801B60A4_OnLoad

key_files.role: Local static header cleanup; removed extern declarations/commented initializer block and added AllstarRoundInfo struct.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gm/gmallstar.static.h added 4 deleted 30 hunks 1
raw_diff: removed extern gm_803DEBE8/gm_80490940 and commented gm_803DEBE8 initializer; added typedef struct AllstarRoundInfo

key_files.role: Symbol/data metadata update; resized gm_803DEBE8 and introduced gm_803DEC4C at .data:0x803DEC4C.
evidence_refs: changed_files: config/GALE01/symbols.txt added 2 deleted 1 hunks 1
raw_diff: gm_803DEBE8 size changed from 0xD0 to 0x64; gm_803DEC4C added as type:object size:0x6C

what_changed: Added includes for gmregcommon.h and melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.h in gmallstar.c to support newly decompiled All-Star code paths.
Moved/defined gm_803DEBE8 as a static 25-entry gm_803DEBE8_t table in gmallstar.c and added static AllstarRoundInfo gm_803DEC4C table.
Added global gm_80490940_t gm_80490940[5] in gmallstar.c.

Replaced stub markers for gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, and gm_801B5ACC with partial C implementations involving All-Star opponent selection, preload/game cache setup, audio preload masks, match setup, match exit handling, and next-round bookkeeping.
Changed gm_801B60A4_OnLoad by removing PAD_STACK(12) and rewriting a byte-zeroing loop from pointer increment form to an indexed for loop.
Updated gmallstar.static.h by adding AllstarRoundInfo and removing data externs/commented table initializer.
Updated symbols.txt so gm_803DEBE8 covers only 0x64 bytes and gm_803DEC4C owns the following 0x6C bytes.

smart_moves.text: The PR split a previously single data symbol into gm_803DEBE8 plus gm_803DEC4C and introduced a small typed struct for the round start/count table, making code references more reviewable than raw offsets.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt gm_803DEBE8 size 0xD0 -> 0x64 and added gm_803DEC4C
raw_diff: gmallstar.static.h added AllstarRoundInfo { u32 start; u32 count; }
raw_diff: gmallstar.c uses gm_803DEC4C[round].start/count

smart_moves.text: The decompilation connected gmallstar.c to existing subsystem APIs rather than leaving all interactions opaque, adding includes and calls for gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable, lbDvd cache helpers, and lbAudioAx preload helpers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added includes gmregcommon.h and melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.h

raw_diff: gm_801B5324 calls gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable, lbDvd_8001822C/lbDvd_80018C6C/lbDvd_80018254/lbDvd_80018C2C/lbDvd_80017700, and lbAudioAx_80026E84/lbAudioAx_80026EBC/lbAudioAx_80026F2C/lbAudioAx_8002702C/lbAudioAx_80027168

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a data blob is used as two logical tables, a symbols.txt split plus a small local struct can clarify code and improve data matching, but the split should remain tied to section/address evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_803DEBE8 size reduced to 0x64 and gm_803DEC4C added at .data:0x803DEC4C size 0x6C
raw_diff: AllstarRoundInfo added and used for .start/.count lookups

decomp_lessons.lesson: Partial decomp progress may still be useful as a handoff base, but bot-reported regressions and author self-critique should keep the result classified as mixed evidence rather than a polished matching pattern.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: author says not the biggest fan and calls it a base for others
text_corpus: bot report shows improvements for four functions but regression for gm_801B60A4_OnLoad

naming_conventions.convention: Kept address-style names for not-yet-semantic globals and functions, while adding a descriptive local type name AllstarRoundInfo for the newly identified start/count table.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added typedef struct AllstarRoundInfo
raw_diff: global symbols remain address-style

naming_conventions.convention: Local variable names use short role names for recovered concepts such as chars, colors, opp_data, allstar, round, color, next_count, opp_start, and opp_idx.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: implementations of gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, gm_801B5ACC

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in newly added functions to influence stack shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added PAD_STACK calls in gm_801B5324/gm_801B5624/gm_801B5ACC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: No narrow objdiff proof for these PAD_STACK uses is present in the slice; current standards require explicit verifier evidence for such tactics before treating them as accepted style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Copied opponent IDs from gm_803DEBE8 into fixed-size char arrays using an 8-at-a-time unrolled loop followed by a remainder loop.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801B5324 count > 8 block with dst[0] through dst[7] assignments and remainder do-while
raw_diff: gm_801B5624 similar count > 8 block with dst[0] through dst[7] assignments and remainder do-while
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This resembles generated or assembly-shaped code. The loaded natural-loops standard says repeated blocks varying by index/pointer/stride should be checked against natural loops before preserving copied generated blocks.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjusted data-symbol ownership in symbols.txt to separate gm_803DEBE8 from gm_803DEC4C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt hunk at gm_803DEBE8/gm_803DEC4C
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is supported by the diff and bot-reported .data improvement, but the slice does not include a full data ownership explanation beyond the changed metadata.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported six unmatched-item improvements: gm_801B5ACC to 79.89%, gm_801B5624 to 78.42%, gm_801B5324 to 74.50%, gm_801B59AC to 72.68%, .data to 96.22%, and .bss to 50.00%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] issue comment 2026-04-20T08:38:56Z

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot also reported a regression in gm_801B60A4_OnLoad from 87.40% to 80.20%, -40 bytes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] issue comment regression table

review_feedback.text: The author said they were not the biggest fan of how the PR turned out and framed it as a base for others to work from.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: issuecomment-4334459894
text_corpus: jurrejelle issue comment 2026-04-28T10:39:02Z

follow_up_queries: gmallstar All-Star mode partial decomp base for others

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2427 metadata, changed files, merged_at
raw_diff: full diff for three changed files
text_corpus: bot report and author comment
changed_files: three changed files with add/delete counts
notes: The slice includes full diff and bot report, so changed files and concrete edits are well supported.
No human review comments are present, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale cannot be inferred.
The bot report gives fuzzy progress/regression percentages but not exact objdiff match closure for the functions.
The author explicitly characterized the work as imperfect and a base for others, which lowers confidence in treating the source shapes as reusable accepted style.
PAD_STACK and unrolled-copy shapes are visible in the diff, but the slice lacks narrow verifier evidence justifying them as necessary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Data-table split plus local struct can make gmallstar partial decomp more tractable
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2427, gmallstar split gm_803DEBE8 in symbols.txt, added gm_803DEC4C, and introduced AllstarRoundInfo so All-Star round start/count accesses could be written as table fields rather than opaque offsets. Treat this as a PR-specific candidate pattern, not a global rule without more examples.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt gm_803DEBE8 size 0xD0 -> 0x64 and gm_803DEC4C added
raw_diff: gmallstar.static.h added AllstarRoundInfo
raw_diff: gmallstar.c uses gm_803DEC4C[round].start/count

accepted_candidate_records.title: Mixed-score partial PRs should retain regression context
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2427 improved several gmallstar unmatched items but regressed gm_801B60A4_OnLoad and was described by the author as an imperfect base. Future curation should preserve both the improvements and the regression/self-critique when using this PR as precedent.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report improvements and gm_801B60A4_OnLoad regression
human_pr_text: author comment calling it a base for others

source_update_candidates.title: gmallstar.c owns All-Star round/opponent data around gm_803DEBE8 and gm_803DEC4C
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2427 changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt so gm_803DEBE8 is .data:0x803DEBE8 size 0x64 and gm_803DEC4C is .data:0x803DEC4C size 0x6C, and defined both in src/melee/gm/gmallstar.c for All-Star mode logic. Curator should verify against current symbols/source before promoting as a path fact.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt data split
raw_diff: gmallstar.c static gm_803DEBE8 and static AllstarRoundInfo gm_803DEC4C
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete ownership/split fact from the PR diff, but path facts must be checked against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK in partial decomp PRs should carry narrow verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2427 contains PAD_STACK uses in newly added partial implementations while the loaded slice only shows fuzzy progress and one regression. This may reinforce the existing matching-tactics standard rather than require a new standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(12), PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(24)
text_corpus: bot report includes improvements and gm_801B60A4_OnLoad regression
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential source-corpus note for curator review; not promoted because the standard already covers this and the PR lacks explicit verifier discussion.

rejection_notes: Do not treat the unrolled 8-at-a-time copy loops as an accepted style lesson; the loaded natural-loops standard suggests such patterns need loop-form checking, and the author did not defend the shape.
Do not claim the four newly implemented functions matched exactly; the bot report only shows partial percentages below 100%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or rationale; the slice has zero review comments and zero reviews.
Do not generalize gm_803DEC4C naming as semantic; it remains address-style despite the AllstarRoundInfo type.
